ask: Users keep missing when their saved jobs get reposted. We should notify them somehow.

story.title: Repost Alerts – Notify User When a Saved Job Is Reposted
story.story: As a job seeker with saved jobs, I want to receive an email notification when a saved job posting is detected as reposted, so that I can reassess the role's legitimacy and decide whether to continue pursuing it.

acs.name: Email Sent After Repost Detected
acs.given: a job seeker has at least one saved job in the `saved_jobs` table
acs.when: the daily ingest batch flags that saved posting as a repost event
thens: The system will send a transactional email to the user's registered address via Resend within 24 hours of the ingest run completing.
The email will identify the job title and employer of the reposted listing.
The email will include a brief, non-alarmist explanation that the posting has been seen before, consistent with calm UI principles.

acs.name: Email Links Back to Saved Job
acs.given: a job seeker has received a repost notification email
acs.when: the user clicks the link in the email
thens: The user will be taken to the relevant posting detail or History page entry within AutoApply.
The ghost-score and its one-sentence rationale will be visible on that page.

acs.name: One Notification Per Repost Event Per User
acs.given: a job seeker has already been notified about a repost event for a specific saved posting
acs.when: the same posting is flagged again in a subsequent ingest run without being unsaved and re-saved
thens: The system will not send a duplicate notification email for that posting to that user.
A new notification will only be triggered if the user unsaves and re-saves the posting, resetting the notification state.

acs.name: User Has No Email Address on File
acs.given: a job seeker's account has no verified email address
acs.when: a repost event is detected for one of their saved jobs
thens: The system will silently skip sending the notification without throwing an error.
The repost event will be logged so the notification can be retried or surfaced in-app in a future iteration.

acs.name: Posting Unsaved Before Ingest Run Completes
acs.given: a job seeker unsaves a posting before the daily ingest batch completes
acs.when: that posting is flagged as a repost in the same ingest run
thens: The system will not send a repost notification for that posting to that user.

assumptions_carried: A1 — Resend is approved as the email provider for this feature, consistent with its use in PM Agent #4; no additional vendor evaluation is assumed.

A2 — A notification-state tracking mechanism (e.g., a `repost_notifications_sent` table keyed on `user_id` + `posting_id` + `repost_event_date`) will need to be created; schema design is left to the developer.
A3 — User email addresses are already stored and accessible in the existing auth/user table; no new email-capture flow is in scope.
A4 — Notification opt-out or preference management is out of scope for this story; a future story should address unsubscribe compliance (e.g., CAN-SPAM).
A5 — The weekly email digest parked in the icebox (C6) is a separate feature and is explicitly excluded from this story.
A6 — In-app / push notifications are out of scope; email is the sole channel for this slice.
A7 — The ghost-score classifier already runs as part of the daily ingest batch and its output is available at the time repost detection occurs.

warnings: AC3: WHEN contains 'and' — likely two ACs.

clarity.reasoning: The generated story uses a named persona ('job seeker with saved jobs'), a clear single-action 'I want' statement, and plain product language. The title is descriptive and unambiguous. Slightly more verbose than the reference but no clarity issues.

testability.reasoning: Most ACs follow GIVEN/WHEN/THEN structure with observable outcomes. AC1 and ACE2 are well-formed. However, the reference includes a daily cap edge case (AC2) with holdover behavior that the generated story omits entirely, replacing it with a duplicate-suppression AC that addresses a different scenario. The generated AC2 is more of a navigation/UI check than a core notification behavior. Missing the daily cap scenario reduces testability coverage.

scope_fidelity.reasoning: The generated story stays within the email notification scope and doesn't invent major out-of-scope features. It adds some implementation detail (Resend provider, table names, schema hints) that edges toward over-specification, but these are flagged as assumptions rather than ACs. The 'calm UI principles' language in AC1 is slightly prescriptive but minor. No compound asks introduced.

completeness.reasoning: The generated story covers more edge cases than the reference (no email on file, unsaved before ingest, duplicate suppression) and has a richer assumptions section. However, it misses the daily notification cap scenario (AC2 in reference) and the posting-deleted-after-detection edge case (ACE2 in reference), which are meaningful gaps. The posting-deleted scenario with skip logging is absent. The additional assumptions (A4 unsubscribe compliance, A5 digest exclusion, A7 ghost-score availability) add useful surfacing of dependencies.

grounding.reasoning: All assumptions are explicitly flagged with A1-A7 prefixes. Claims reference specific system components (Resend, saved_jobs table, daily ingest batch, ghost-score classifier). Unverified assumptions are clearly labeled rather than stated as facts. The story ties back to context (PM Agent #4, icebox item C6) showing grounding in product context. No ungrounded claims presented as facts.
